# Questions in Livestream 191

Live Stream #191: Happy New Year!!!

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/J_S_LxHFa08?t=432" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">7:12</a> | In Genesis 19:1, is Lot's bowing a form of worship? | He said it may be, though the context does not make that completely certain; at minimum it could be more than ordinary respect. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/J_S_LxHFa08?t=951" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">15:51</a> | Were Amorites, Hittites, Mitanni, and related peoples Indo-European? | He said only the Hittites in that list were Indo-European; the others were not. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/J_S_LxHFa08?t=1110" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">18:30</a> | How did the wandering Israelites have so many animals available to sacrifice? | He said they were shepherds and had animals with them, and that Israelite slavery in Egypt was state-owned labor rather than chattel slavery. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/J_S_LxHFa08?t=1574" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">26:14</a> | How should we understand that no one can see God when the Father is seen in Daniel 7? | He said Daniel 7 is a vision and not the same as directly entering God's throne room or seeing God unmediated. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/J_S_LxHFa08?t=3153" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">52:33</a> | Why did God have Moses take off his sandals? | He said the text identifies the ground as holy, and the sandals may represent ordinary uncleanness or animal material inappropriate for sacred space. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/J_S_LxHFa08?t=4231" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:10:31</a> | What are good ways to stop misinformation from Ron Wyatt, David Down, and others in the church? | He said it is difficult once people already believe the misinformation, so the better approach is patient correction before bad claims take hold. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/J_S_LxHFa08?t=4800" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:20:00</a> | Could Philippians 2:9-11 support universal reconciliation, since confessing Jesus as Lord requires the Holy Spirit? | He examined the passages but did not take them as a straightforward proof of universal reconciliation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/J_S_LxHFa08?t=5772" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:36:12</a> | If Kitchen showed Old Testament laws resemble thirteenth-century BC Hittite documents, why would the author favor Hittite over Egyptian or Canaanite models? | He said the issue is not direct borrowing from Hittite documents, but that surviving Hittite treaty forms are the comparative evidence we have. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/J_S_LxHFa08?t=6090" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:41:30</a> | Is sola scriptura "Scripture alone" from an authority perspective rather than a source perspective? | He said the slogan is mainly about final authority, not a claim that no other sources may be consulted. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/J_S_LxHFa08?t=6564" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:49:24</a> | What is your favorite book on the history of Jesus and the resurrection? | He said New Testament resurrection evidence is not his specialty, so he was not the best person to recommend a favorite book. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/J_S_LxHFa08?t=6858" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:54:18</a> | Why is Rebekah's nurse Deborah important enough to have her death noted? | He said nurses could be significant household figures, but the text gives limited information and does not let us reconstruct much more. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/J_S_LxHFa08?t=7645" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:07:25</a> | What theological problems do you see with purgatory? | He argued it starts from the wrong premise, because sin is not something humans can personally work off after death if Christ's grace already covers it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/J_S_LxHFa08?t=8188" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:16:28</a> | How was the shekel amount from the census calculated, and does it imply 600,000 people? | He called it a major unresolved question and treated the census figures as part of the broader population-number problem. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/J_S_LxHFa08?t=8905" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:28:25</a> | Was Eglon's residence in Judges 3:13 an Iron Age I Jericho city villa or a nearby site? | He said the question is debated, with evidence for later Iron Age I settlement but not enough certainty for a firm identification. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/J_S_LxHFa08?t=9859" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:44:19</a> | Was Jericho a major city, and was its destruction layer flattened by an earthquake? | He described Jericho as more like a fortified town and said the later leveling was from repeated rebuilding activity, not evidence for an earthquake. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/J_S_LxHFa08?t=10398" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:53:18</a> | What is `abrek` in Genesis 41:43? | He said the term occurs outside Genesis and reviewed Egyptian lexical evidence, but treated the precise interpretation as debated. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/J_S_LxHFa08?t=11223" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:07:03</a> | Is the rock highlighted in recent videos really the split rock of Moses? | He said no, because the geology, lack of a spring, and location all work against that identification. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/J_S_LxHFa08?t=12344" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:25:44</a> | Should Joel Kramer's Exodus material be trusted? | He said he would not trust Kramer on the Exodus because Kramer approaches it from an ABR perspective and lacks the relevant terminal training. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/J_S_LxHFa08?t=12650" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:30:50</a> | How would you divide periods in a real-time strategy game about ancient warfare? | He said the divisions would need to be culture-specific, because labels like Bronze Age and Iron Age describe material culture rather than uniform military periods. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/J_S_LxHFa08?t=12803" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:33:23</a> | What is Gideon's ephod, and is it different from the high priest's breastplate? | He said yes; an ephod can be a front-hanging garment or object, not only the high priestly breastplate. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/J_S_LxHFa08?t=13429" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:43:49</a> | Why do experts with PhDs sometimes ignore research or new data? | He said some stop reading, stop learning, or cling to inherited academic traditions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/J_S_LxHFa08?t=14330" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:58:50</a> | Under Occam's Razor, is the resurrection as a group hallucination the most logical explanation? | He said no; group hallucinations are rare and tend to produce inconsistent accounts, so they do not fit the resurrection evidence well. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/J_S_LxHFa08?t=14863" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:07:43</a> | Were early Jewish marriages mainly legal agreements? | He said yes, largely, and noted that this was common across much of the ancient world. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/J_S_LxHFa08?t=15158" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:12:38</a> | What similarities did Egyptian priestly rituals have with Israelite rituals, and did they share the same theology? | He said there were some similarities, such as ritual bathing and priestly shaving, but the theology was not the same. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/J_S_LxHFa08?t=15460" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:17:40</a> | Did Israelites always keep their beards, or did some shave? | He said they were expected to keep beards, though they could trim them rather than grow them without limit. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/J_S_LxHFa08?t=15585" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:19:45</a> | Have you seen the channel Metatron, and what do you think of his Egypt videos? | He generally likes Metatron's work in his strongest areas, but said Egypt is not his main expertise and is only okay. |
